pr.title: V3: Interactive review + knowledge graph
pr.body:
## Summary

- **Interactive review UI** — Papers tab gets a per-paper Include/Exclude toggle, reviewer notes, and a full details expander (problem, method, contributions, key results, datasets, limitations). Clusters tab gets inline editable labels. All changes are persisted immediately to `outputs/<run_id>/review.json`.
- **Regenerate flow** — new `papertriage regenerate <run_id>` command and viewer button reruns only synthesize + critique with review overrides applied (excluded papers filtered out, cluster labels replaced). Output lands in `outputs/<run_id>/regenerated_<timestamp>/`; the original run is never modified. Inherits the original run's critic mode from `meta.json`.
- **Knowledge graph** — `src/papertriage/graph/builder.py` builds a NetworkX cosine-similarity graph from embeddings and renders it as a self-contained pyvis HTML file.

diff --git a/.gitignore b/.gitignore
index 456ab7b..c34a79f 100644
--- a/.gitignore
+++ b/.gitignore
@@ -29,4 +29,7 @@ test_papers/
 # OS / editor
 .DS_Store
 .idea/
-.vscode/
\ No newline at end of file
+.vscode/
+
+# pyvis JS bundles written alongside the script that calls save_graph()
+lib/
\ No newline at end of file
diff --git a/pyproject.toml b/pyproject.toml
index 8bb834e..f768185 100644
--- a/pyproject.toml
+++ b/pyproject.toml
@@ -4,7 +4,7 @@ build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"
 
 [project]
 name = "papertriage"
-version = "0.3.0"
+version = "0.4.0"
recent_merged_titles: feat: arXiv adapter, extraction caching, and bug fixes (v0.2.0)
docs: add shield badges for Python version, license, coverage and status
Portfolio polish: MIT license, example run output, viewer screenshots
Phase 3: Viewer, Evals, README, plus targeted fixes
Phase 2: Pipeline — clustering, synthesis, critique, orchestration, CLI
Phase 1: Foundation — scaffolding, LLM client, extraction pipeline
